description: A section displaying a collection of course highlight statistics or selling points, each with a title and description paragraph.

props.name: stats
props.type: Array<{ iconClass: string; title: string; description: string }>

stories.name: Default
stories.description: Course stats section with multiple highlight items

stats.title: Top 20 in the UK for Chemistry
stats.description: The Times and Sunday Times Good University Guide 2026

stats.title: 1st in the Russell Group for learning opportunities, student voice, and assessment and feedback in chemistry
Royal Society of Chemistry (RSC) accredited course
stats.description: This course is accredited by the RSC for fully meeting the academic criteria for Chartered Chemist (CChem).

stats.title: Opt to spend a full year on an industrial work placement
stats.description: Test out a career path, build up your CV and grow your network of contacts.

stories.name: LongDescriptions
stories.description: Course stats with longer narrative descriptions rather than short stat lines

stats.title: Learn from experts in the field
stats.description: You'll be taught by academics who are at the forefront of their fields, contributing to cutting-edge research that shapes the future of chemical engineering.

stats.title: World-class facilities, practical training
stats.description: Gain invaluable experience using industrial-scale equipment in energy, pharmaceutical, and biological engineering.

stats.title: Our graduates work in leading chemical engineering roles
stats.description: Our course is designed with your future in mind, with employability embedded at every stage.

notes: Each stat item is a Drupal paragraph entity with classes 'paragraph paragraph--type--ug-statistic paragraph--view-mode--default'. The stat-title h4 always contains a Font Awesome icon (i.fas.fa-star) followed by the title text. The icon class is always fa-star in all observed samples but is modelled as a prop for flexibility. The heading h3 is always present and always reads 'Why study this course?' but is modelled as an optional prop with that default. All Drupal paragraph wrapper classes must be preserved verbatim in the rendered output.
